relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.
textual_note: John 7:53-8:11 is treated as a later received tradition and kept bracketed in the restored layer.

verses.mystical_translation: A later tradition places Jesus on the Mount of Olives.
At dawn he teaches again in the temple.
A woman is placed in the middle as a test-case.
Her accusation is made public.
Moses is invoked as a trap.
Jesus answers accusation by writing on the ground.
The stone can only be cast by the one without misalignment.
He returns to the ground-writing silence.
The accusers leave one by one.
Jesus asks where condemnation has gone.
He releases without condemning and calls her onward.
Jesus reveals himself as light of the world.
The Pharisees challenge his witness.
He knows origin and destination; they do not.
Human-surface judgment cannot read him.
His judgment is not solitary but with the Father.
The Law’s two-witness pattern is invoked.
The Son and Father bear witness together.
Knowing Jesus and knowing the Father belong together.
He teaches in the treasury, untaken because the hour has not come.
He speaks again of going where they cannot come.
They misunderstand through death rather than origin.
Above and below, not-this-world and this-world, are contrasted.
Trusting that I am becomes the passage out of dying in misalignment.
They ask who he is; he points back to what he has said from the beginning.
He speaks into the world what he hears from the true Sender.
They do not understand he speaks of the Father.
When the Son of Humanity is lifted, I am will be known.
The Sender is with him because he does what is pleasing.
Many trust as he speaks.
True disciples remain in his word.
Truth known becomes freedom.
They appeal to Abraham and deny bondage.
Practiced misalignment is bondage.
The enslaved one does not remain in the house like the son.
The Son’s freedom is true freedom.
Abrahamic seed is not enough while the word has no room.
Jesus speaks what he has seen with the Father.
Children of Abraham would do Abraham’s works.
Seeking to kill the truth-speaker is not Abraham’s way.
They claim the Divine as Father.
If the Divine were Father to them, love for the Sent One would follow.
They cannot hear his word.
Adversarial parentage is revealed by desire for violence and falsehood.
Truth itself becomes the reason they do not trust.
If truth is spoken, refusal to trust is exposed.
Being from the Divine is shown by hearing divine words.
They answer with insult: Samaritan and spirit-troubled.
Jesus refuses the accusation and honors the Father.
He does not seek his own glory.
Keeping his word opens life beyond death’s sight.
They hear the death claim as impossible because Abraham and prophets died.
They ask who he makes himself to be.
The Father is the one who glorifies him.
He knows the Father and keeps the Father’s word.
Abraham rejoiced to see his day.
They misunderstand through ordinary age.
Before Abraham came into being, I am.
Stones rise, but Jesus is hidden and leaves the temple.
